review_text:
The good- there were a lot of parking spaces in the parking lot and the decor was festive.
The bad- we ordered a double fajita for me and my wife to share. It cost us 27 dollars but we were excited because we thought we'd have a nice meal. When our food came we were surprised as their double was the size of other restaurant's single for which we would have paid 12-18 dollars for . Thefood was not the best tasting fairly bland it was not sizziling like a fajita should be. The shrimp were cold on one side and only had grill marks on the other.  We ordered a fried ice cream which was hard as a rock with a chewy outer layer that did not look cooked and it was in a cold taco salad style tortilla bowl.
The ugly- we lodged a complaint with our waitress who didn't seem to care about our concerns and insisted everything was as it should be.
Over-all- the service was not impressive the food was worse. The Los Tres Amigo's in Howell Michigan would get a 5 star from me but this one didn't come close. Go to taco Bell for a better Mexican meal experience .

review_text:
As a recommendation, I ordered the Quesabirria and it was probably the greasiest, least flavorful tacos I have ever had. I am not sure if they are deep fried or fried on a black top grill but they were just greasy. The beef had no flavor and I actually had to add fresh jalapeno to the tacos for any type of flavor. The tacos were served room temperature as well. The sauces that came with it were just as bad. The green sauce was like baby food and bland, the red sauce was as if someone added hot sauce and sugar to ketchup, and the other sauce (consommé?) that is served in a larger ramekin was oily with added onion and cilantro. The only reason that I finished my meal was it was the only thing that I had all day minus some strawberry yoghurt for breakfast. Thank God for the chips and salsa that were on the table. Maybe make the salsa less sweet, because it tasted like salsa from a container from Sysco or US Foods.
Mind you, I was with a party of about 15 but there should be no excuse for sitting at a restaurant for at least 2 hours before receiving our meals. The service was so bad that we saw people just get up and leave after sitting at their own tables for a while. I felt bad for our waitress telling us that they were understaffed, and she was supposed to already be off of her shift so she was trying to do the best that she could, but I wouldn't want my staff voicing their frustrations to customers.
I have tried Los Tres Amigos before, and honestly this place is not any better than the first time. I love Tex-Mex and Mexican food being that I am originally from Texas, so I grew up eating this type of food weekly and I also make it from scratch at home (no kits, cans or packaged seasoning). I can honestly tell you that I would not recommend or even classify Los Tres Amigos as Tex-Mex or Mexican. I am being harsh on this but this is my honest opinion on spending over $50 for 2 meals.

review_text: I've eaten Mexican food from California to Michigan!..Food and price was within range of most Mexican restaurants of this caliber!.!..We came up from Northville!..It was 2pm!.. She wanted a drink it was happy hour!..I don't drink but I like to eat!..First thing I look for on the menu is Ceviche!.Nadda!!..To me it's not an authentic Mexican restaurant unless it has Ceviche!..Period!..Anyway after the disappointment..I ordered  the fajita burrito and my friend ordered the Verde burrito extra spicy!..!!..Our server pointed to  the bottled  hot sauce on table!..We looked at each other like O.K.!..To her and I extra spicy means to me.. add some extra heat to the ingredients while preparing!..We order Chinese food extra spicy all the time they know how to do it!..So other than the house sauce served with chips being bland..the extra hot sauce was good after we asked if there was a hotter sauce..there was they brought out from the back..it made up for a lot!..It's my friends and her parents Mexican to go to place!..Will I go back!!..No!..Not until  I see Cevice  on the menu!

We didn't think the food had a lot of flavor .. It was our 1st time here ... I ordered tacos al carbon, with refried beans, pico and special sauce ... My husband ordered an enchilada dinner with rice and refried beans ...
The chips are thick hard salt less corn tortilla chips ... salsa has a little heat after eating some,  didn't really taste the tomato... The refried beans were creamy, but little flavor ... pico was pretty good ... on my tacos it said cilantro and onion, they weren't kidding, gobs of both ... The carbon steak meat wasn't seasoned but juicy and had quite of bit of lean meat ... husband said the rice was good ...
My order was incorrect when they brought it to the table ... they did bring my correct order ... my bill wasn't changed to my correct order,  I brought it to there attention and it was corrected ... when we went it wasn't crowded 2:30pm .. Decor is  like other Mexican restaurants in my area ... we were from out of town .... If I  lived local,  I  don't think we'd go back ... too bland for my taste ...

Great service and an delicious food! The tacos were large and filling, more so than what I generally think of tacos. Great meat! The chorizo was a bit over cooked, but still had great flavor. We were also impressed with their COVID safety precautions. Overall we had a fantastic time and agreed that we were overall impressed with the experience and food.
I have loved this place for 20 years now. My first visit was in Jackson since then they have popped up in numerous places.

review_text:
This is my absolute go-to restaurant. I’d say we’ve been here probably 2-3 times a month for at least the last year and it’s never once disappointed.

Both my husband and I have a standing order— a chimichanga (I prefer the chicken, he gets the shredded beef) and an order of the queso fundido. Service has always been great (ironically the only time it was off was the first time we came but the food was good enough to bring us back and the waitstaff has been amazing since) and the restaurant is incredibly clean.

Their lunch hours are, I believe 11am - 2pm (possibly starts at noon?) and we generally try to go then but we’ve even stopped in an hour before closing on weekdays for just apps and dessert and were still happy with our visit. The soda can be a little persnickety so I generally get water but my husband enjoys their fountain drinks.

I know they have a large selection of alcoholic beverages but we’re not a drinking family so I can’t comment on that. I will say I often see other tables with fancy, pretty looking drinks so they must be of a good quality as well. We have kids who don’t enjoy Mexican cuisine and they’ve ordered the chicken tenders and fries 3+ times and each time, commented on how it’s “real chicken” and really enjoyed their meals.

Another problem, and this is likely not legal, they charged us sales tax on the tip amount they added to our bill. You cannot tax a service, and if you're being charged a tip, it's for service, so it can't be taxed.

The chicken was dry but overall the meal wasn't too bad, though my husband and I did agree that the plates were hotter than the food.
The service was atrocious, the waiter didn't know how to speak up, we could barely understand what he was saying and when he brought us a new basket of chips, he didn't take the old one away, he just set the new basket on top of the old basket. The floors and tables were sticky af, and we felt the prices were a little steep based on the quality of the food. I'm not sure if the poor service is what made the food not good,  or if it was actually just not great food
Honestly,  we could have gotten better service and food from the new Taco Bell in Corunna.

I honestly believe that the manager could make this a nice place to eat if he/she could find responsible, caring employees but, we all know finding those two traits in one person, especially when it comes to the service industry hardly exists. But, if he/she were able to find them, he/she could probably turn his/her restaurant around.
owner_response:

We have given this restaurant multiple tries after new management took over. We had come here every Friday for almost 2 years until the shift. The atmosphere changed drastically. When the manager was asked to come over due to a messed up order we were immediately met with a rude and condescending individual. The waitresses later apologized for his actions which speaks volumes to the chain of command. We thought maybe ordering out would be the better option but upon arrival to pick up our order they said it was never received when it had been confirmed by them and the meal paid for. CASSANDRA the waitress was extremely rude, she saw us standing there for 10 min before coming over with a disgruntled tone and attitude. She didn't offer alternatives or assistance and when we asked if we could re-place the order we were met with backlash. She stated no manager was there and she was only a waitress so we would have to contact our email for the problem. I was willing to repay for the order as it was our meal plan and we had no plan B but she continued to be rude. Needless to say we left even more in awe of the lack of compassion and service this restaurant continues to display. Take my advice and go 10 minutes down the road to El Patrero. It will save you.

When he took our order, I said I wanted the steak fajitas and he asked me if I wanted the single fajitas and I said no I wanted the lunch fajitas. Then when it came time to check out instead of the lunch purchase price I was charged about six or seven dollars more and the receipt said single fajitas. He refused to adjust the price to what I ordered and kept arguing with me so I finally said fine I just won’t leave you a tip. review_text: Absolute shame what this restaurant has come to.  Doordashed a fajita Amigos dinner today and I barely got 3 fajitas out of it.  And one of them was pure vegetables....for $30.  Food was flavorless and bland and lacked basic fajita seasoning.  There use to be a time when restaurants took pride in their food and service, as most still do.  Los Tres Amigo doesn't.  As a matter of fact, they're completely opposite.  My wife picked the chicken out of her fajitas because it tasted old.  Did the taste test and sure enough, tasted like 2...3 day old chicken.  Only 3 pieces of shrimp and barely any beef.  I guarantee you it was one of the owners who made my order and he had one thought on his mind...make it small and save money.  The rice was cold (order was delivered 6 minutes after they picked it up) and the refried beans were as runny as diarrhea.  The entire order was a complete failure of epic proportions...not the first time, but I gave them another chance.  We were hungry.  I will never step foot in this place or order out again.  As a matter of fact, I'm rooting more for this place to fail and close the doors than anything else.  You can't screw your customers the way they did me today.  I guarantee this is a regular habit of theirs and other customers also feel my pain.  Not another dime...

review_text:
Absolutely terrible experience at the Owosso, MI Los Tres Amigos location. Our group of 26 waited nearly 2 hours with very little service, barely any communication, and long stretches without even water refills. We were patient and understanding the entire time, but after asking if anything could be done on the bill due to the experience, the owner, Jesse, came out yelling and cursing at our group instead of trying to resolve the situation professionally.

I simply asked if he could do better than 15% off considering how poorly everything was handled, and he responded by escalating the situation and calling the police. I have never experienced an owner speak to customers that way in my life.

I understand restaurants get busy, but there is absolutely no excuse for treating customers with hostility and disrespect. Extremely disappointing experience and not somewhere I would recommend after the way we were treated.
